vocab.example: I strictly allocate a budget for footwear each year to avoid overspending on luxury brands.

vocab.meaning: món đồ mua sắm xa hoa/phung phí
vocab.example: Spending five hundred dollars on a single pair of sneakers would be considered an extravagant purchase for me.
I'm willing to pay more if the durability factor is high, as it saves money in the long run.

vocab.meaning: một con số/số tiền gây kinh ngạc (lớn)
vocab.example: For some sneakerheads, spending a staggering amount on limited editions is just part of the hobby.

vocab.meaning: giá trị sử dụng thực tế
vocab.example: I tend to focus on the utilitarian value of shoes rather than the prestige of the brand name.

vocab.meaning: thu nhập khả dụng (tiền dư ra sau khi trừ các chi phí thiết yếu)
vocab.example: I only buy high-end dress shoes when I have some extra discretionary income at the end of the quarter.

vocab.meaning: món đồ mang tính đầu tư (chất lượng tốt, dùng được lâu dài)
vocab.example: I view a good pair of leather boots as an investment piece that will last for a decade.

vocab.meaning: người luôn săn tìm hàng giá rẻ/giảm giá
vocab.example: As a bit of a bargain hunter, I rarely pay full price, preferring to wait for major seasonal clearances.

vocab.meaning: tỉ lệ giữa giá cả và chất lượng
vocab.example: I always research the price-to-quality ratio before committing to a pair of expensive running shoes.

vocab.meaning: việc mua sắm ngẫu hứng (không kế hoạch)
vocab.example: Most of the shoes in my closet were an impulse buy because they happened to be on sale when I walked past the store.
